description_long: This pennant features a triangular banner with a golden-yellow upper section adorned with a red emblem resembling a lion or mythical creature, and a lower section in a light blue shade. The banner is bordered with a dark outline and is suspended from a horizontal black bar, which is attached to a vertical pole. The pole is segmented with alternating dark and gold bands, giving it a decorative, ceremonial appearance. At the top of the pole, there is a stylized finial with a geometric design, while the bottom terminates in a solid black base. The pennant appears to be made of fabric for the banner and metal or wood for the pole and mounting hardware. This object is likely intended for display in public urban spaces, such as along streets or at the entrance to parks or plazas.
description: A decorative triangular pennant with a gold and blue banner featuring a red emblem, mounted on a segmented pole with a geometric finial and base. The design is ceremonial and suited for urban display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full triangular banner with gold and blue sections and a red emblem, as well as the decorative pole and finial.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pennant, with the pole and banner appearing almost edge-on and minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the back of the banner and pole, but with less detail on the emblem.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the narrow edge of the pennant and pole.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant displays a gold upper section with a red lion-like emblem and a blue lower section, all bordered in black. The design is simple and heraldic, with no text.
